inline_comments.message: Thoughts? Should I reinstate an existingModuleReferences? Could be done programtically via lookup or by creating another query to essentially get the opposite of the unknowns
I'm looking into bulk updates rather than individual which wouldn't the most optimal if we have e.g. 10,000 cm handles
It has to be reinstated but how?
this should NOT be input to the method!

existingModuleReferencesFromCmhandle 

You now need to deduce these  removing 'identified new ones' from  moduleReferencesFromCmHandle

I think you can actually use Collection.removeAll()! Probably better to use stream and collectors the correct way.

moduleReferencesFromCmhandleAlreadyInCps  = moduleReferencesFromCmHandle.stream(.!in(newModuleReferencesFromCmHandle)   ).collect(toCollection)

should not be part of any interface

sinetad create somethign like 

Collection<....> identifyNewYangResourceModuleReferences(Collection<....> inputYangResourceModuleReference)

remove known ones (they are in the DB)

should NOT need this!

NCMP get from node ALL module refences on a given Cm Handle :  [A,B,C,D,E]
CPS-Core will tell NCMP which one are NEW
newModuleReferences = cpsModuleService.identifyNewYangResourceModuleReferences([A,B,C,D,E])
newModuleReferences -> [C,E]
-> existingModuleReferencesForCmhHandle = [A,B,D]
as discussed this code all belongs to ModuelReference Repository (just mater of setting up 2nd interface for that and move this impl there)

see comment on uniqueness of this name in native query impl part.

so method will take in list of modulesfrom cm handle, create temp tabel do thee join (thsi query) with existing modules and return the 'new' ones....

Amm..I guess table names in postgres would be limited to 63chars. 
This UUID bit after replacement would be 32 chars. So are we sure the moduleReferencesToCheck length would not be greater than 31 chars??
I think this should be OK as the UUID is 32 hex digits + 4 dashes. Without the dashes it will be 32 as you said. So 32 + moduleReferencesToCheckTemp (28) = 60 chars total

I answered this in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/127208/26/cps-ri/src/test/groovy/org/onap/cps/spi/impl/CpsModulePersistenceServiceIntegrationSpec.groovy where the existing are tested elsewhere.

If there are no moduleReferences then the query formed will be invalid (as for-each section wont be executed)
It wont pollute the data as nothing would be inserted [bad query] , just that there will be exceptions related to that.
